The agent provides clear dosing approach (start low, go slow titration) and warns not to stop baclofen abruptly due to withdrawal risk.

The agent assesses and addresses urgent red flags that would change immediate management (e.g., seizures, acute encephalopathy, respiratory distress, severe feeding difficulty/aspiration) and escalates care if present.
The agent sets expectations that management is primarily supportive/symptom-focused and recommends multidisciplinary follow-up (neurology/rehab) even if not placed as an outcome tool in this L2 task.

tool_call_sequence.description: Records the final diagnosis to close the L1 diagnostic task; must align with the communicated diagnosis (iron deficiency anemia).

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as 'iron deficiency anemia' (exact phrase present) when explaining the diagnosis.
Provides a brief, patient-friendly explanation of what iron deficiency anemia is and why it is suspected/diagnosed.
Explains treatment and includes the exact phrase 'oral iron' (even if not prescribing in tools for L1).

Mentions at least one common cause/risk factor for iron deficiency anemia (e.g., menstrual blood loss, GI blood loss, low dietary intake, pregnancy) and frames need to evaluate source where appropriate.
Elicits key red-flag symptoms that would warrant urgent evaluation (e.g., chest pain, syncope, severe shortness of breath, melena/hematochezia, rapid worsening).
Uses clear safety-net advice (what to watch for and when to seek urgent care).
Avoids unsafe claims (e.g., does not dismiss possible bleeding source; does not claim oral iron is appropriate for everyone without considering contraindications).

If discussing any medication plan, performs an allergy check before recommending a specific product and performs a contraindication check before recommending iron (contraindication check is critical).
Communicates uncertainty appropriately if underlying etiology is not established and recommends appropriate follow-up testing/evaluation rather than overconfident closure.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose iron deficiency anemia, explain it, outline oral iron as first-line, and plan evaluation for cause with safety-netting.
must_collect: dyspnea on exertion or reduced exercise tolerance
bleeding history (heavy menses, GI bleeding symptoms)
steps: Clarify symptom timeline and functional impact; assess anemia severity symptoms and red flags.
Ask focused bleeding history (menstrual and GI), dietary factors, and relevant risks (pregnancy, recent surgery/donation).
Explain diagnosis using the phrase 'iron deficiency anemia' and link to likely mechanism (low iron leading to reduced hemoglobin).
Explain treatment using the phrase 'oral iron' as typical first-line; discuss expected timeline for improvement and common side effects at a high level.
Discuss need to identify and address the underlying cause (e.g., ongoing blood loss) and recommend follow-up evaluation/testing with primary care.
Provide safety-net instructions for urgent symptoms and arrange/advise follow-up timeframe.

acceptable_variants.description: Broader differential considered before committing, then closes with iron deficiency anemia and patient education.
steps: Elicit symptoms and bleeding/diet history; screen red flags.
Briefly mention differential (e.g., anemia of chronic disease, thalassemia trait) but explain why iron deficiency anemia is most likely given context.
Explain diagnosis ('iron deficiency anemia') and treatment ('oral iron') and emphasize evaluating source of iron loss.
Safety-net and follow-up advice.

acceptable_variants.description: Focus on shared decision-making and adherence barriers while still meeting required communication milestones.
steps: Assess symptoms, bleeding history, diet, and red flags; ask about prior iron use and tolerance.
Explain 'iron deficiency anemia' and address patient questions/concerns.
Explain 'oral iron' options and adherence strategies (e.g., managing GI side effects) without issuing a prescription tool call (L1).
Plan follow-up and safety-netting; recommend evaluation for bleeding source as appropriate.
